title: Systems and methods of ice making devices
abstract: An ice making device includes an evaporator assembly and an ice box (6). A member (301) of the evaporator is inserted into an ice mold, and the ice box can be driven by a lifting device. The first end of the ice box includes an ice outlet, and the second end of the ice making box includes an ice pushing device. The ice pushing device horizontally pushes the ice particles on the ice making box to the ice outlet. The up-and-down ice making box and horizontal ice pushing device push the ice particles on the ice making box horizontally to the ice outlet.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. An ice maker, comprising: an ice mold; an evaporator comprising one or more members positioned to be received in the ice mold to cause water in the ice mold to freeze into ice; a pusher positioned to push the ice out of an outlet of the ice mold; and a drive assembly coupled with the evaporator to move one or more members of the evaporator out of the ice mold and coupled with the pusher to drive the pusher to push the ice out of the outlet. 2. The ice maker of claim 1, wherein the drive assembly comprises a first motor coupled with the evaporator and a second motor coupled with the pusher. 3. The ice maker of claim 1, wherein the drive assembly is to move the evaporator linearly away from the ice mold. 4. The ice maker of claim 1, wherein the drive assembly is to move the pusher linearly along or over the ice mold. 5. The ice maker of claim 1, wherein: a plurality of members of the evaporator comprise the one or more members; and the ice mold comprises a plurality of openings aligned coaxially with the plurality of members to receive the plurality of members, the plurality of openings coupled with a water tank to receive the water. 6. The ice maker of claim 1, wherein the ice mold is structured to form the ice as bullet ice or pellet ice. 7. The ice maker of claim 1, further comprising a controller coupled with the evaporator to cause the evaporator to operate in a deicing mode. 8. The ice maker of claim 1, wherein the drive assembly is to move the evaporator in a vertical direction and to move the pusher in a horizontal direction subsequent to lifting of the evaporator away from the ice mold. 9. An ice making apparatus, comprising: an evaporator comprising a plurality of fingers coupled with a refrigerant source, the plurality of fingers arranged in parallel; an ice mold comprising a plurality of openings positioned coaxially with the plurality of fingers; a pusher aligned with ice mold; a first motor to cause movement of the evaporator in a first direction relative to the ice mold; and a second motor to cause movement of the pusher in a second direction relative to the ice mold. 10. The ice making apparatus of claim 9, further comprising: a water tank storing water; and a pump coupled with the water tank and the ice mold to pump water from the water tank into the plurality of openings. 11. The ice making apparatus of claim 9, wherein the first motor is to move the evaporator vertically away from the ice mold. 12. The ice making apparatus of claim 9, wherein the second motor is to move the pusher linearly along or over the ice mold. 13. The ice making apparatus of claim 9, wherein: a plurality of members of the evaporator comprise the one or more members; and the ice mold comprises a plurality of openings aligned coaxially with the plurality of members to receive the plurality of members, the plurality of openings coupled with a water tank to receive the water. 14. The ice making apparatus of claim 9, wherein the ice mold is structured to form the ice as bullet ice or pellet ice. 15. The ice making apparatus of claim 9, further comprising a controller coupled with the evaporator to cause the evaporator to operate in a deicing mode responsive to detection of completion of an ice formation period. 16. The ice making apparatus of claim 9, wherein the pusher comprises a net. 17. An ice making system, comprising: an evaporator; a mold facing the evaporator; a motor to move the evaporator, in a first state, towards the mold, and to move the evaporator, in a second state, away from the mold; and a controller comprising one or more processors to: control operation of the evaporator to cause water in the mold to freeze in the first state; and control operation of the motor to cause the movement of the evaporator according to the first state and the second state. 18. The ice making system of claim 17, wherein the controller is to cause a pump to provide the water to the mold. 19. The ice making system of claim 17, wherein the controller is to cause the evaporator to operate in a deicing mode prior to causing the motor to move the evaporator away from the mold. 20. The ice making system of claim 17, wherein the controller is configured to cause a member to be driven across at least one of the mold or a housing coupled with the mold to drive the ice out of an outlet.
